name: Nmap Basics - First Recon
story: You are starting your first shift as a junior security analyst. Before touching a real target, you must learn how to perform safe local reconnaissance inside a controlled sandbox. The host intentionally exposes a small number of services. Your job is to verify that Nmap is available, try the most common scan modes, compare their outputs, and produce a clean report of the open ports and services on localhost.
objectives: Learn core Nmap commands, understand common scan modes, identify open ports on localhost, and recognize exposed services.

steps.title: Verify Nmap
steps.description: Confirm that Nmap is installed in the environment before starting your reconnaissance.
steps.question: What command confirms your Nmap version?

hints.text: Use the Nmap command with the standard version flag.

steps.title: Read the help
steps.description: Inspect the built-in help page to understand the main command families exposed by Nmap.
steps.question: Which command displays the built-in help page for Nmap?

hints.text: Most CLI tools expose a short help page with a single flag.

steps.title: Run a basic scan
steps.description: Perform a default TCP scan against localhost.
steps.question: Which command performs a basic scan on localhost?

hints.text: Use the IPv4 localhost address with the default Nmap command.

steps.title: Scan specific ports
steps.description: Limit the scan to the two most relevant ports for this lab: SSH and HTTP.
steps.question: Which command scans only ports 22 and 80 on localhost?

hints.text: Use the option that lets you provide a custom list of ports.

steps.title: Detect service versions
steps.description: Run a version detection scan to identify the services behind the open ports.
steps.question: What command enables service version detection on localhost?

hints.text: Use the Nmap option dedicated to service version detection.

steps.title: Use aggressive detection
steps.description: Try the aggressive scan mode to combine version detection and default script execution.
steps.question: Which command launches an aggressive scan on localhost?

hints.text: Nmap has a short option that bundles several advanced probes.

steps.title: Report exposed services
steps.description: Read the scan result and identify the services exposed on the local host.
steps.question: List the open ports and services in this format: 22/tcp ssh, 80/tcp http

hints.text: Look for the lines marked open in the Nmap output.
The lab starts an SSH service and a Python HTTP server.
